name: Vendor Contract Register Analyzer
description: Extracts the structured register fields from a single vendor contract. Generic across clients: descriptions tell the model how to read each field, never what any specific contract says. Pairs with contract-register-schema-v1 (14 columns). source_contract and confidence_flag are NOT extracted here: source_contract comes from file metadata, and confidence_flag is derived downstream from CU's native per-field confidence + estimateFieldSourceAndConfidence output.

vendor.description: The vendor / service-provider company name as written on the contract. This is the counterparty supplying the goods or services, NOT the client/customer.

client_name.description: The client/customer company name exactly as written, preserving the legal suffix (e.g. 'LLC' or 'Inc.') and punctuation. Do not normalize, expand, or standardize the suffix.

service_category.description: The single primary service category for the core function this vendor provides. Pick the best-fit category. Use 'other' only when none apply.

annual_value.description: The stated annual total fee as a plain number, no currency symbol or thousands separators (e.g. 54000). Read the explicit annual total the contract states (e.g. 'totaling USD $54,000 annually'). Do NOT compute it from a per-unit or per-seat rate times a quantity, and do NOT sum monthly figures yourself. If the contract states only a monthly or other periodic fee with no stated annual total, leave this null and put the figure in annual_value_note. If there is no fixed periodic fee (variable, hourly, or usage-based), leave this null.

annual_value_note.description: Short context ONLY when annual_value cannot be cleanly resolved to a single stated annual number (e.g. a variable/usage-based fee, or only a monthly figure is stated). Leave empty whenever a clean stated annual total exists.

currency.description: The ISO currency code of the contract amounts (e.g. USD).

renewal_type.description: How the contract renews. 'auto' = renews automatically unless cancelled within a notice window. 'manual' = requires an affirmative action to renew. 'none' = a fixed term that simply ends with no renewal mechanism.

renewal_date.description: The next renewal or expiry date of the contract.

notice_days.description: The required non-renewal / cancellation notice period expressed in days (e.g. 30, 60, 90). If the contract states the period in weeks or months, convert to a number of days.

escalation_type.description: The type of price increase applied on renewal. 'CPI' = tied to an inflation / consumer-price index. 'fixed-percent' = a stated fixed percentage. 'usage' = tied to consumption or volume. 'none' = no price-increase clause.

escalation_pct.description: The fixed percentage increase when escalation_type is 'fixed-percent' (e.g. 5 for a 5% increase). Leave null for any other escalation type.

has_dpa.description: Whether the contract carries a data processing addendum (DPA), a HIPAA business associate agreement (BAA), or an equivalent written data-privacy / data-protection obligation. A general confidentiality or security clause alone does NOT count: answer 'no' when there is no specific data-processing/privacy obligation.
